mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity sell-offs over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A declining industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, forcing short-term equity drawdowns as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum produces positive 5-day forward returns in 60% of late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation above 230 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.04 against 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into durable demand for materials and financial services, pushing quarterly equity returns higher.
failure_condition: HOUST levels show no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding the 100 baseline threshold signals restored manufacturing capacity and positive output surprises, generating immediate positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO crosses above 100.
